req: REQ-026 (Platform Task Management (Linear as system of record))

issues.detail: AT-026.02 makes the NGO assistant a PM-mirror consumer, while AT-026.03 says the panel is the NGO's only Linear-visibility surface. The updated requirement contains the same unresolved contradiction.
issues.fix: Define the panel as the only structured tree surface while explicitly allowing the NGO-only read-only assistant, or remove task data from the assistant; align both tests and the requirement.

issues.detail: The pre-gate clause says the PM tree carries only its kickoff seed, which can be read as only the bootstrap task; REQ-026 and AT-026.01 require scope-requirement items plus the bootstrap at kickoff.
issues.fix: State explicitly that before the gate the dev tree is empty while the PM tree retains all kickoff-seeded scope requirements plus the bootstrap item.

issues.detail: The test requires each unspecified Linear event to appear in “the mirror.” If dev-tree events are included, this conflicts with the PM-only mirror consumed by NGO surfaces; if they are excluded, the three fixtures are underspecified.
issues.fix: Distinguish internal Linear-event ingestion from the NGO-facing PM projection. Scope actor/provenance assertions by tree and prove that dev events needed internally never enter the panel or assistant projection.

issues.detail: A worktree is bound to a PM requirement, not a single dev issue. Requiring all task-state operations to affect only “its own issue” can reject legitimate operations on multiple linked dev items within that requirement.
issues.fix: Assert isolation of PM authority and attribution to the bound PM requirement, while allowing vendor-native operations on its linked dev items and forbidding cross-worktree PM contamination.

issues.detail: These retain single-tree vocabulary: coordinator push-assignment of “build tasks” and an inactive generic “issue.” They can pass against dev items without proving the PM-tree pull and stale-pull rules.
issues.fix: Retarget AT-026.12 to absence of coordinator push-assignment for PM requirements. Define AT-026.14 against a pulled PM requirement and specify whether activity is aggregated from its linked dev work; test dev-only behavior separately if intended.

issues.detail: The test assumes PM requirements have blocking relations, but REQ-026 only explicitly encodes blocking relations on dev-tree decomposition. It therefore sidesteps how linked dev dependencies determine whether a PM requirement is pullable.
issues.fix: Specify the PM blockedness oracle—explicit PM relations or deterministic roll-up from linked dev relations—and test that exact mechanism, including a dev dependency that blocks the corresponding PM pull.

issues.detail: The dev self-assignment negative proves only that PM status is unchanged; it does not prove that a dev item cannot create, replace, or become the attribution binding. The d82 model binds pulled PM requirements only.
issues.fix: Assert that dev self-assignment, status changes, and merges leave the worktree binding on the pulled PM requirement and that metered usage never binds to a dev item.

issues.detail: The verified-completion gate lacks decisive negative edges: one linked dev item still open, acceptance evidence missing or attached to another pull, and dev leaves manually marked Done without merges. The last case is especially important because manual dev status changes are allowed to stand.
issues.fix: Add negatives proving PM Done requires all linked dev work actually merged plus matching acceptance evidence recorded against the same active pull; vendor-native or manual dev Done alone must not authorize PM Done.

issues.detail: The PM-tree bootstrap item has no tested lifecycle. It must carry authoring attribution before the dev tree exists, but the stated PM completion predicate requires linked dev work to be merged, which the bootstrap cannot have.
issues.fix: Define and test the bootstrap authority explicitly—for example pull to In Progress and PRD merge plus gate-pass evidence to Done—or redefine it so the general verified-completion predicate applies.

issues.detail: Lifecycle tests still refer to generic issues and a singular final tree. They do not establish whether cancellation, abandonment reset, and preservation apply to PM items, dev items, or both, nor how those actions coexist with dev-tree revert exemption.
issues.fix: Use separate PM and dev fixtures for completion cancellation and abandonment return. Specify whether both trees are preserved; if only the PM projection is snapshotted for NGO visibility, say so explicitly.

issues.detail: A single dev status-change probe does not exclude dev item titles, hierarchy, relations, comments, or activity events from every NGO surface. Dev activity could still leak into recent activity or assistant answers while the status probe passes.
issues.fix: Probe dev entity presence and metadata across the panel, assistant, dashboard/activity feed, and notifications, while confirming the corresponding PM data remains visible.

issues.detail: The coverage map admits that the required recent-activity presentation has no acceptance test and cites no concrete cross-suite test. This leaves an explicit REQ-026 acceptance criterion unverified.
issues.fix: Add a timestamped PM-activity test, including recency/order and a negative dev-activity fixture, or cite an existing exact cross-suite AT that proves all of those behaviors.

issues.detail: Active PRD text outside REQ-026 remains single-tree: Story 5, REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-028, and Technical Considerations still make self-assignment the In-Progress authority and matching PR merge the sole Done path for the visible task tree. These are not superseded historical notes.
issues.fix: Retarget those sections to PM pull → In Progress and verified requirement completion → Done, with dev items using vendor-native status and merge-to-Done. Update Skill binding and completion language to PM requirements only.

issues.detail: Story 3 and REQ-036 still say kickoff creates exactly one bootstrap task, omitting the kickoff-seeded PM requirement items, while REQ-016 sends generic task-status notifications to the NGO and can leak dev-tree events.
issues.fix: Clarify that only the dev backlog is empty pre-gate while the PM tree contains scope requirements plus bootstrap, and scope NGO task notifications strictly to PM-tree events.
